title: finishing-a-development-branch

goal.text: Complete finished development work through the correct integration menu, with test gating, environment detection, and provenance-aware cleanup.

requirements.summary: Use this only after implementation is complete and you need to decide whether to merge, open a PR, keep the branch, or discard the work.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests are verified before any integration options are offered.
The workflow distinguishes normal repo or named-branch worktree from detached HEAD.
The menu preserves the source option families and their safety constraints.
Cleanup only happens on the source-approved paths and only when worktree ownership allows it.

tasks.title: Verify tests before offering any completion options
tasks.detail:
Run the relevant test suite before presenting merge, PR, keep, or discard options. If tests fail, stop and report the failures instead of proceeding.

Use [CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md](/D:/Users/chany/Documents/claw-kit/packages/codex-adapter/skills/finishing-a-development-branch/CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md) for the required stop conditions.

tests-pass.summary: Tests passed; it is safe to move into environment detection.
nextsteps: Determine repo and worktree state before presenting choices.
Do not skip the environment branch.

tests-failing-stop.summary: Closeout is blocked until the failing tests are fixed.
nextsteps: Do not offer merge or PR paths yet.

tasks.title: Detect environment and resolve the menu shape
tasks.detail: Determine whether this is a normal repo, a named-branch worktree, or a detached-HEAD worktree. That decision controls which menu can be shown and whether cleanup is ever allowed.

nextsteps: Resolve the base branch if merge is possible.
Present the exact standard menu without extra explanation.

detached-head-menu.summary: Use the reduced detached-HEAD menu with no local merge option.

tasks.title: Resolve base branch if needed and record the user's option
tasks.detail: If local merge is available, determine or confirm the base branch. Then present the exact source menu and record the user's selected path.

nextsteps: Merge first, verify the merged result, then clean up if ownership rules permit.

push-and-pr.summary: The user chose push and PR creation.
nextsteps: Push the branch and create the PR.
Preserve the worktree for future iteration.

keep-as-is.summary: The user chose to keep the branch as-is.
nextsteps: Report the preserved branch and worktree path.
Do not clean up the workspace.

discard.summary: The user chose the discard path, which requires explicit confirmation.
nextsteps: Require typed discard confirmation before deleting anything.
Only then apply cleanup and branch deletion in the correct order.

tasks.title: Execute the selected path with the correct cleanup rules
tasks.detail: If tests failed, stop and report that closeout cannot continue. Otherwise execute the selected option safely: merge and re-verify before cleanup, push and create PR while preserving the worktree, keep the branch untouched, or require typed `discard` confirmation before destructive cleanup. Only remove worktrees for merge or discard, only from the main repo root, and only when the worktree path proves this workflow owns it.

references.why: Preserves the exact branch families, safety rules, and cleanup ownership checks from the source skill.
Keeps the full original command examples, menu wording, and cleanup details beside the converted entry.
Maps source information to the converted homes for verification and maintenance.

rules: Do not offer integration options before test verification succeeds.
Do not continue past failing tests; stop and report them.
Detect environment before presenting the menu, and preserve the exact detached-HEAD restriction.
Present the exact option families from the source workflow rather than an open-ended question.
Do not delete work without typed discard confirmation.
Do not clean up the worktree for PR or keep-as-is paths.
Do not remove host-owned worktrees or run worktree removal from inside the worktree being removed.
Re-verify tests on the merged result before branch deletion on the local-merge path.
